raw_consumer_statement: On or about XX/XX/XXXX, my credit card was charged for purchases made at XXXX that I didn't make. The card is a Citi Mastercard. Citi sent me a letter on XX/XX/XXXX, yesterday, that it was closing the investigation into this episode. The letter stated that XXXX informed Citi that the items purchased were purchased by me and sent to my home. Although one of the items was sent to my home, the label on the package had the name XXXX XXXX on it. I do not know this person. A photo of the package is attached, below. The item in question was a bag of red plastic cups. The other item is a picnic table and umbrella. This item did not get delivered to my home. Both items sum up to {$97.00}. After receiving the letter from Citi informing me that Citi was closing its investigation and charging me {$97.00}, I immediately called XXXX. The XXXX employee with whom I spoke said that XXXX had no record of my having made these purchases and confirmed that these purchases were made fraudulently using my credit card. So why is Citi closing the investigation? Based on my conversation with several employees at XXXX, XXXX does NOT maintain that I made these purchases or that they were both delivered to my home. Although the red plastic cups were. Clearly, Citi is closing an investigation that should remain open and doing so under false pretenses.
